candidate_profiles.name: Product Craft Workbench
candidate_profiles.summary: A product-surface register for useful app screens: clear task hierarchy, restrained composition, tactile details, and one memorable product-specific gesture only when it helps the work.
fit_signals: App UI, dashboards, settings, score viewers, editors, and repeat workflows
Impeccable-style product register rather than brand register
OpenAI frontend-skill restraint: no default card grids, no hero-card reflex, one dominant idea per section
Anthropic frontend-design specificity: choices grounded in the product subject, not generic SaaS styling
UI UX Pro Max coverage: accessibility, interaction, layout, typography, color, and data states

judgment_axes.hierarchy: task-first hierarchy with one clear working focus, using layout, type weight, and proximity before decorative emphasis
judgment_axes.density: moderate-to-dense where repeat work benefits, with breathing room reserved for orientation, reading, and complex decision points
judgment_axes.tone: precise, capable, tactile, and subject-aware without drifting into marketing polish
judgment_axes.motion: subtle state and spatial continuity only; motion must clarify affordance, selection, or transition

candidate_profiles.name: Brand Signal Editorial
candidate_profiles.summary: A brand-surface register for first impressions, landing pages, and narrative content where the page needs a distinctive visual thesis before it needs operational density.
fit_signals: Marketing, launch, portfolio, or public-facing explanatory pages
OpenAI frontend-skill image-led hierarchy and first-viewport product clarity
Anthropic frontend-design signature element and deliberate type/palette choices
Higher tolerance for expressive imagery, asymmetry, and scroll narrative
Useful when the impression is part of the product

judgment_axes.hierarchy: first-screen thesis with unmistakable subject signal, strong visual anchor, and sections that each do one job
judgment_axes.density: spacious but purposeful, with copy and visual assets carrying clear narrative progression
judgment_axes.tone: distinctive, editorial, confident, and specific to the subject rather than broadly premium
judgment_axes.motion: noticeable but curated; one entrance, one scroll/depth moment, and one affordance transition only when they reinforce the story

candidate_profiles.name: Dense Ops Console
candidate_profiles.summary: A high-throughput register for tables, queues, diagnostics, review screens, and expert flows where compact comparison and repeat action matter most.
fit_signals: Tables, bulk actions, filters, logs, queues, and status-heavy surfaces
Expert or repeated operational use
UI UX Pro Max accessibility and interaction checks take priority over expressive novelty
Compact controls, clear active state, and strong information scent
Useful when throughput and recovery paths matter more than visual drama

judgment_axes.hierarchy: fast-scanning landmarks, persistent controls, and clear current-state indicators
judgment_axes.density: dense by default, but never cramped below readable type, target size, or recovery affordance requirements
judgment_axes.motion: minimal feedback for loading, selection, expansion, and destructive confirmations; no decorative motion

candidate_profiles.name: Music Visual Instrument
candidate_profiles.summary: A subject-led register for music theory, fretboard, chord, score, and listening surfaces where notation, rhythm, and instrument metaphors can shape the interface.
fit_signals: Songs, scores, chords, fretboards, interval displays, playback, and teaching tools
Anthropic frontend-design subject grounding: visual choices come from musical materials and use cases
A signature interaction can be justified when it behaves like an instrument or notation aid
Visual systems may encode pitch, time, selection, repetition, or harmonic function
Useful when the interface should feel playable or readable, not merely administrative

judgment_axes.hierarchy: musical structure drives visual hierarchy: sections, measures, voicings, playback state, and selected harmonic objects
judgment_axes.density: variable by mode, from readable score spacing to compact practice controls, with responsive fit checked at the working pane
judgment_axes.tone: focused, rhythmic, legible, and instrument-like with craft in notation details
judgment_axes.motion: timing-aware and functional, especially for playback, selection, progression, and learning feedback

notes: The old warm restrained profile set has been discarded; do not use it as the default aesthetic lens.
Use Impeccable's brand-surface versus product-surface distinction as the first routing decision for new profiles.
Use OpenAI frontend-skill hard rules as a restraint check: avoid default cards, hero cards, clutter, weak first-screen product signal, and sections with multiple competing ideas.
Use Anthropic frontend-design as a process reference: ground palette, typography, layout, and any signature element in the actual subject and audience.
Use UI UX Pro Max as a coverage reference for accessibility, interaction, layout, typography/color, navigation, and data visualization checks.
Anthropic public design skills are external references in this repository unless they appear in the active Codex skill registry.

anti_pattern_candidates.description: A first screen or primary workspace is made from interchangeable cards, generic stats, and centered SaaS composition instead of a subject-specific layout.
A product workflow is styled like a marketing page, or a brand surface is flattened into a dense app shell without a clear first impression.
A distinctive visual or motion idea is added because the design needs personality, but it does not help the subject, task, or user decision.
Animations compete for attention, fail reduced-motion expectations, or do not clarify hierarchy, continuity, feedback, or affordance.
Components introduce one-off hex values, spacing, radius, or shadows that bypass the declared token and review layers.
A surface is made spacious or compact by taste alone, without matching the user's task frequency, reading load, or comparison needs.
Contrast, focus, keyboard access, touch targets, text fit, or state recovery are treated as cleanup after visual styling instead of core design constraints.
Music-learning or notation surfaces ignore musical structure and render as generic records, forms, or dashboards.

open_questions: Should runtime CSS tone variables be replaced to match Product Craft Workbench, or should they stay as legacy tokens until a browser-verified visual task is approved?
Which profile should own the public home page if the repository needs both a product app and a marketing entry point?
Should Music Visual Instrument become the selected default if songs, scores, and chord workflows remain the product center of gravity?
How much visual signature is acceptable inside dense product screens before it slows repeat work?
Should external design skills be installed locally, or should this repository continue treating them as cited references only?
